code_changes.content:
## Terraform v1.15.0 (Unreleased)

### Breaking Changes

- **backend/s3**: The `AWS_USE_FIPS_ENDPOINT` and `AWS_USE_DUALSTACK_ENDPOINT` environment variables now only respect `true` or `false` values, aligning with the AWS SDK for Go v2. Previously, any non-empty value was treated as `true`. Update your environment variable exports to use explicit `true` or `false` values instead of relying on implicit truthy behavior. ([#37601](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/37601))

### New Features

- **command/validate**: The `validate` command now checks the `backend` block for errors. This ensures the backend type exists, that all required attributes are present, and that the backend's own validation logic passes. This allows validation failures in backend configuration to be detected earlier without running `terraform init`. ([#38021](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/38021))

- **core/variables**: You can now set a `deprecated` attribute on `variable` and `output` blocks to indicate that they are deprecated. Terraform will produce warnings when a value is passed to a deprecated variable or when a deprecated output is referenced. This allows module authors to guide users toward using newer input/output names. ([#38001](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/38001))

- **backend/s3**: The S3 backend now supports authentication via AWS SSO using `aws login`. This enables users to leverage their AWS CLI SSO configuration directly within Terraform without manually managing long-lived credentials. ([#37967](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/37967))

- **build**: Terraform now produces official builds for Windows ARM64 (`terraform_windows_arm64`), enabling support for ARM-based Windows environments. ([#32719](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/32719))

### Enhancements

- **provisioner/remote-exec**: SSH-based provisioners (file and remote-exec) now re-enable support for PowerShell on Windows systems. This restores the ability to use PowerShell scripts within remote-exec provisioners on Windows targets. ([#37794](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/37794))

- **command/init**: The `terraform init` command now includes millisecond precision in log timestamps, improving the ability to correlate logs with other systems that use millisecond precision. ([#37818](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/37818))

- **command/init**: The `terraform init` command now skips dependencies declared in development overrides. This allows you to use `terraform init` with developer overrides and install dependencies that are not declared in the override file, enabling more flexible local development workflows. ([#37884](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/37884))

- **moduletest**: Terraform Test now allows function calls within mock blocks. Previously, mock blocks only supported literal values; they can now use built-in functions to construct more complex mock data. ([#34672](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/34672))

- **lang/deprecated**: Detection of deprecated resource attributes and blocks has been improved, providing more accurate warnings to users when they use deprecated schema elements. ([#38077](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/38077))

### Bug Fixes

- **moduletest/junit**: File-level error diagnostics are now included in JUnit XML output for skipped test elements. This ensures CI/CD pipelines can properly detect and report validation failures that occur during Terraform test operations. ([#37801](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/37801))

- **command/refresh**: Fixed an issue where a refresh-only plan could result in a non-zero exit code despite having no changes to report. The exit code now correctly reflects whether changes were detected. ([#37406](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/37406))

- **command/show**: Fixed a crash in `terraform show -json` that occurred when a plan contained ephemeral resources with preconditions or postconditions. The JSON serialization now handles these special resource types correctly. ([#37834](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/37834))

- **command/init**: Fixed `terraform init -json` to properly format all backend configuration messages as JSON instead of mixing plain text and JSON. All output is now consistently formatted as JSON when `-json` is specified. ([#37911](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/37911))

- **command/state**: The `state show` command will now explicitly fail with exit code 1 when it cannot render the state for named resources. This provides clearer feedback when the command encounters issues, rather than potentially succeeding with partial output. ([#37933](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/37933))

- **command/apply**: Terraform will now raise an explicit error if a plan file intended for one workspace is applied against another workspace. This prevents accidental application of plans to the wrong workspace, which could lead to unintended infrastructure changes. ([#37954](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/37954))

- **core/lifecycle**: The `replace_triggered_by` meta-argument now validates attribute references and reports an error when given an invalid reference that does not exist in the target resource. This prevents silent failures and provides clearer error messages during plan. ([#36740](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/36740))

- **backend**: Fixed a nil pointer dereference crash during `terraform init` when the destination backend returns an error. The initialization process now handles backend errors gracefully without panicking. ([#38027](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/38027))

- **stacks**: Fixed an issue where stacks did not send progress events when a plan failed. This improves UI integration for Terraform stacks by ensuring complete event reporting even in error scenarios. ([#38039](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/38039))

- **cloud**: Terraform Cloud and registry discovery network requests are now more resilient, with improved error handling for temporary network or service-related failures. This reduces the frequency of transient errors during operations that depend on cloud connectivity. ([#38064](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/38064))

- **stacks**: Fixed a UI inconsistency where component instances in convergence destroy plans were not reporting no-op plan/apply operations correctly. Component instances now properly report when no changes are needed. ([#38049](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/38049))

### Experiments

Experiments are only enabled in alpha releases of Terraform CLI. The following experimental features are not yet available in stable releases:

- **Deferred Actions** (`-allow-deferral`): This experimental feature permits `count` and `for_each` arguments in `module`, `resource`, and `data` blocks to have unknown values. It allows providers to react more flexibly to unknown values by deferring operations that cannot proceed without complete information.

- **Terraform Test Cleanup** (`terraform test cleanup`): In experimental builds, when test operations fail, Terraform saves a manifest file and state files for each failed cleanup operation in the `.terraform` local directory. The experimental `test cleanup` command attempts to clean up these local state files automatically without requiring manual intervention.

- **Terraform Test Backend Blocks and Skip Cleanup**:
  - Test authors can now specify `backend` blocks within `run` blocks. Run blocks with backend blocks will load state from the specified backend instead of starting with empty state, allowing test infrastructure to remain active between test operations.
  - Test authors can specify `skip_cleanup` attributes in test files and run blocks. State files for affected run blocks are written to disk in the `.terraform` directory and can be cleaned up manually using the experimental `test cleanup` command.

### Previous Releases

For information on prior major and minor releases, refer to their changelogs:

- [v1.14](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.14/CHANGELOG.md)
- [v1.13](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.13/CHANGELOG.md)
- [v1.12](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.12/CHANGELOG.md)
- [v1.11](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.11/CHANGELOG.md)
- [v1.10](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.10/CHANGELOG.md)
- [v1.9](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.9/CHANGELOG.md)
- [v1.8](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.8/CHANGELOG.md)
- [v1.7](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.7/CHANGELOG.md)
- [v1.6](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.6/CHANGELOG.md)
- [v1.5](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.5/CHANGELOG.md)
- [v1.4](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.4/CHANGELOG.md)
- [v1.3](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.3/CHANGELOG.md)
- [v1.2](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.2/CHANGELOG.md)
- [v1.1](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.1/CHANGELOG.md)
- [v1.0](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.0/CHANGELOG.md)
- [v0.15](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v0.15/CHANGELOG.md)
- [v0.14](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v0.14/CHANGELOG.md)
- [v0.13](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v0.13/CHANGELOG.md)
- [v0.12](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v0.12/CHANGELOG.md)
- [v0.11 and earlier](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v0.11/CHANGELOG.md)

conversation_events.text:
# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

**Target Repository:** `github.com/sg-benchmarks/terraform--a3dc5711`
- Use `repo:^github.com/sg-benchmarks/terraform--a3dc5711$` filter in keyword_search
- Use `github.com/sg-benchmarks/terraform--a3dc5711` as the `repo` parameter for go_to_definition/find_references/read_file


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# Task: Generate Terraform Changelog

**Repository:** github.com/sg-benchmarks/terraform--a3dc5711 (mirror of hashicorp/terraform)
**Output:** Write your changelog to `/workspace/CHANGELOG.md`

## Objective

Generate a structured changelog entry for Terraform covering significant changes in the repository. Analyze the commit history, existing CHANGELOG.md, and source changes to produce a well-categorized changelog.

## Scope

Analyze the repository to identify:
- **New features**: New commands, configuration options, provider capabilities
- **Bug fixes**: Resolved issues with state management, plan/apply correctness, or CLI behavior
- **Breaking changes**: Any changes that require user action or break backward compatibility
- **Deprecations**: Features or behaviors being phased out
- **Performance improvements**: Changes that improve plan/apply speed or memory usage

Focus on changes visible in the `internal/`, `command/`, and `backend/` directories.

## Output Format

Write to `/workspace/CHANGELOG.md` using Terraform's established format:

```markdown
## [Unreleased] / next release

### Breaking Changes

- **[component]**: Description of breaking change and migration path

### New Features

- **[component]**: Description of new feature with usage example if applicable

### Bug Fixes

- **[component]**: Description of the bug and what was fixed

### Performance Improvements

- **[component]**: Description

### Deprecations

- **[component]**: What is deprecated and what to use instead
```

## Quality Bar

- Every entry must reference a specific component (e.g., `backend/s3`, `command/apply`, `internal/states`)
- Breaking changes must include a migration path or workaround
- At least 3 entries per category that has changes
- Do not fabricate entries — base everything on actual code/commit evidence

## Anti-Requirements

- Do not copy the existing CHANGELOG.md verbatim
- Do not include changes that are not evidenced in the codebase
I'll help you generate a Terraform changelog by analyzing the repository. Let me start by exploring the repository structure and understanding existing patterns.
Now let me search for recent code changes and analyze the source code in key directories to identify what has changed:
Let me search more broadly for recent changes in specific components:
Let me explore the `.changes/` directory to see all the categorized changes:
Now let me read all the changelog entries to build a comprehensive changelog:
Let me search for any additional changes that might not be in the yaml files yet:
Now I have all the data I need. Let me create a comprehensive changelog file based on all the changes I've identified:

Perfect! I've successfully generated a comprehensive Terraform changelog and written it to `/workspace/CHANGELOG.md`. Here's a summary of what was created:

## Changelog Summary

The generated changelog for **Terraform v1.15.0 (Unreleased)** includes:

### Breaking Changes (1)
- **backend/s3**: AWS environment variables (`AWS_USE_FIPS_ENDPOINT`, `AWS_USE_DUALSTACK_ENDPOINT`) now only accept explicit `true`/`false` values, aligning with AWS SDK v2 behavior.

### New Features (4)
- **validate command**: Now validates backend blocks for configuration errors
- **Variables/Outputs**: Can set `deprecated` attribute to warn users when deprecated values are used
- **S3 Backend**: Support for AWS SSO authentication via `aws login`
- **Build System**: Windows ARM64 builds are now officially available

### Enhancements (5)
- **Provisioners**: SSH-based provisioners re-enable PowerShell support on Windows
- **Init Logging**: Millisecond-precision timestamps
- **Development Overrides**: Skip dependencies not in override files
- **Module Testing**: Allow functions in mock blocks
- **Deprecation Detection**: Improved detection of deprecated resource attributes

### Bug Fixes (11)
- JUnit XML reporting for test validation failures
- Refresh-only plans exit code handling
- JSON serialization of ephemeral resources with conditions
- `terraform init -json` format consistency
- `state show` error handling
- Plan file workspace validation
- `replace_triggered_by` attribute validation
- Backend error handling during init
- Stack progress event reporting
- Cloud network resilience improvements
- Stack component no-op reporting

### Experimental Features
- Deferred Actions (unknown counts/for_each)
- Test Cleanup command
- Test backend blocks and skip cleanup options

All entries are **evidence-based** with direct references to GitHub issue numbers from the actual changelog entries found in the `.changes/v1.15/` directory. Each entry includes a specific component identifier and context about what changed and why.

### Breaking Changes
